mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the equity discount rate and boosts risk appetite, so month-over-month cuts predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months when FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month, or if the sign relation flips during easing periods for any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a recent baseline signals weakening household income and demand, which raises recession risk and depresses S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the trigger loses predictive sign in any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both accelerate together, sales growth is being matched by payroll creation, indicating broad demand strength that supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition on RSAFS and PAYEMS does not outperform each series alone in out-of-sample sign accuracy, or if simultaneous growth fails to yield positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO indicates slowing industrial supply and weaker cyclical earnings momentum, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if negative monthly changes in INDPRO are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the effect is absent across a full 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST below its recent trend marks a housing-demand rollover that precedes weaker consumer activity and lower S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 8% versus its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the housing decline trigger is not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 60% of events, or if the sign is not stable across two consecutive annual subsamples.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of high FEDFUNDS and elevated CPIAUCSL inflation indicates restrictive policy pressure; this should compress valuation multiples and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation above its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-rate/high-inflation condition fails to produce negative 20-day SPX returns in the majority of triggered months, or if a single-variable FEDFUNDS rule matches or exceeds its predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in PAYEMS after recent weakness signals labor-market stabilization and improved earnings resilience, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises for 2 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if two-month payroll rebounds do not lead to positive 5-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the signal is offset by higher unemployment in the same window.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is positive while UNRATE is falling, real activity is broadening faster than labor slack is rising, and S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDP and falling unemployment do not jointly correspond to positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of available quarters, or if the interaction term becomes insignificant in any 3-year rolling window.
